sub_problems: How should a long-running agent tag specific pieces of internal state (skills, episodic facts, environment models, transient goals) so that a downstream recycling/cleanup subsystem can selectively act on them?

What real-time signals (token-budget pressure, latency budget, anomaly rate) should trigger the agent to enter a cleanup phase, and what governs the transition out of cleanup back into productive work?

How should the agent route different content types through distinct recycling pathways (e.g., compress-and-keep vs. discard-after-summary vs. break-up-into-reusable-fragments) rather than a single uniform pruning rule?
How can we measure agent health longitudinally via the ratio of usable-to-stale internal state, beyond end-task success metrics?
